title: DSDS Use Case Definitions
description: Definitions for documenting when to use and when not to use a design system artifact. Use cases provide scenario-driven guidance that complements guidelines by framing decisions in terms of concrete situations.

useCase.description: A single use case scenario describing a situation in which an artifact is or is not appropriate.

alternative.description: The recommended alternative artifact for this scenario. Typically provided for whenNotToUse entries to direct the reader toward a more appropriate component or pattern.

name.description: The name of the recommended alternative component or artifact (e.g., 'link', 'anchor', 'icon-button').

rationale.description: An explanation of why the alternative is more appropriate for this scenario. Cite semantic differences, accessibility implications, or UX rationale when available.

examples.description: Optional examples illustrating the use case scenario.

useCases.description: Scenario-driven guidance describing when an artifact is and is not the right choice. Complements guidelines by framing decisions around concrete user or developer situations rather than abstract rules.

whenToUse.description: Scenarios in which this artifact is the appropriate choice.

whenNotToUse.description: Scenarios in which this artifact should not be used. Each entry SHOULD include an `alternative` pointing to a more appropriate artifact or pattern.
